params.text:
First Aid Awareness

Emergencies can happen anywhere — in someone's home, in a care setting, or out in the community. Knowing what to do in the first few minutes can keep a person alive until professional help arrives. This course builds your confidence to recognise a medical emergency, take safe and sensible action, and summon help.

Important: this is awareness training only. It gives you an overview of common first-aid situations, but it does not qualify you as a first aider and does not replace a hands-on, certificated first-aid course (such as Emergency First Aid at Work). Always follow your employer's policies and get accredited practical training before you act as a designated first aider.

What a first aider does

A first aider is the person who gives immediate help to a casualty before the emergency services take over. The three overall aims are often summarised as the "three Ps":

  • Preserve life — your own, the casualty's and any bystanders'.
  • Prevent the situation getting worse — stop the condition deteriorating.
  • Promote recovery — comfort, reassure and hand over to professionals.

Above all: stay calm, keep yourself safe, and never put yourself in danger to reach a casualty. You cannot help anyone if you become a second casualty.

params.question: Completing this awareness course qualifies you to act as a workplace first aider.

l10n.wrongAnswerMessage: Not quite. This is awareness only — it does not replace a practical, certificated first-aid qualification.
l10n.correctAnswerMessage: Correct. Awareness training gives you useful knowledge, but you still need a certificated hands-on first-aid course to be a designated first aider.
